data.title: Assessing Investment AB Latour (OM:LATO B) Valuation After Mixed Q1 2026 Earnings Results
data.description:
Investment AB Latour (OM:LATO B) is back in focus after reporting Q1 2026 results. The company reported lower sales and net income year on year, while pointing to solid organic growth, robust margins and continued acquisition activity.

See our latest analysis for Investment AB Latour.

The Q1 update appears to have weighed on sentiment, with a 7 day share price return showing a 6.38% decline and a year to date share price return showing a 5.62% decline. The 1 year total shareholder return of a 19.4% decline points to fading longer term momentum, despite recent 30 day share price support represented by a 4.02% gain.

If Latour’s latest move has you reassessing your watchlist, this is a good moment to broaden your search and check out 97 top founder-led companies

With weaker Q1 earnings, a softer share price and solid organic growth in the background, you now need to ask: is Latour quietly trading below its potential, or is the market already pricing in the next leg of growth?

Advertisement

Preferred P/E of 29.8x: Is it justified?

Latour currently trades on a P/E of 29.8x, above both its Swedish and wider European industrial peers. This suggests the market is paying a premium for its earnings.

The P/E multiple compares the share price with earnings per share, so a higher figure usually reflects investors being prepared to pay more for each unit of current profit. For an investment company with long term holdings in industrial businesses, this often comes down to how durable those earnings look and how confident the market is that they can be maintained or gradually improved.

Here, the premium multiple sits alongside a few mixed signals. Earnings over the past year declined by 9.8%, net profit margins eased from 18.7% to 16.1%, and return on equity of 10.5% is assessed as low relative to a 20% benchmark. At the same time, revenue is forecast to grow by 4.1% per year, which is ahead of the Swedish market forecast of 0.5% per year. The company is also flagged as having high quality earnings and modest 1.3% annual earnings growth over the past 5 years.

Compared with the European Industrials industry average P/E of 21.2x, Latour’s 29.8x looks clearly more expensive. That gap implies investors are accepting a richer price than for many peers, even though recent earnings momentum has cooled and the SWS DCF model currently values the shares at SEK112.25, below the last close of SEK209.8.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

However, you also need to weigh risks such as a 19.4% 1-year total return decline and a P/E premium that could unwind if sentiment or earnings soften further.

While the 29.8x P/E suggests Latour is on the expensive side, the SWS DCF model paints an even starker picture. It shows an estimated value of SEK112.25, compared with a last close of SEK209.8. If the cash flow assumptions hold, is the share price leaning too far ahead of fundamentals?

data.title: Is Penumbra (PEN) Pricing In Too Much Growth After Recent Trial Developments
data.description:
• Wondering if Penumbra at around US$325.33 is offering you real value or just a rich price tag? This article walks through what the latest numbers actually say about the stock.
  • The share price shows a 1.4% decline over the last 7 days and a 1.1% decline over the last 30 days, while returns stand at 5.1% year to date and 9.9% over the past year.
  • Recent coverage around Penumbra has focused on its position in the medical equipment space and how investors are weighing growth potential against risks. This context helps explain why the stock’s moves have been relatively measured rather than sharply one sided.
  • Right now, Penumbra scores 0 out of 6 on Simply Wall St’s valuation checks, as shown in its valuation score. The next sections will walk through different valuation approaches and then finish with a framework that can help you interpret that score in a more practical way.

Penumbra scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Penumbra Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting them back to today using an appropriate rate. It is essentially asking what all those future cash flows are worth in today’s dollars.

For Penumbra, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow stands at about $199.9 million. Based on analyst inputs and further projections by Simply Wall St, forecast Free Cash Flow runs from $156.6 million in 2026 to $442.6 million in 2035, all in dollars. These ten year cash flows are discounted back to arrive at an estimated intrinsic value per share of about $180.37.

Compared with a recent share price around $325.33, this DCF outcome suggests the stock is around 80.4% above the model’s fair value estimate, so the shares screen as expensive on this cash flow basis.

Result: OVERVALUED

Approach 2: Penumbra Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings, which makes it a straightforward cross check against the DCF view. A higher P/E can reflect strong growth expectations or lower perceived risk, while a lower P/E can point to weaker growth prospects or higher risk, so what counts as a “fair” P/E depends on these factors.

Penumbra currently trades on a P/E of about 72x. That sits well above the Medical Equipment industry average of about 23.6x and also above the peer average of about 29.7x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Penumbra is 27.94x, which is its proprietary estimate of what a “normal” P/E might look like after accounting for the company’s earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and risk factors.

This Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple comparison with peers or the industry, because it adjusts for company specific characteristics rather than assuming all medical equipment stocks should trade on the same multiple. Set against the current P/E of 72x, the Fair Ratio of 27.94x points to Penumbra trading at a richer level than this framework would suggest.

Result: OVERVALUED

They update automatically when fresh information such as earnings results or trial data like STORM PE or STRIKE PE comes through, so your Fair Value and thesis stay aligned with what is happening at the company.

Most Popular Narrative: 79.5% Undervalued

Compared with Beam’s last close at $30.70, the most widely followed narrative assigns a fair value of $150.00 per share, pointing to a very wide gap between price and perceived potential.

The "Molecular Pencil": Why Beam's Technology is Built to Win The investment thesis in Beam Therapeutics is a long-term, high-conviction bet on a fundamental technological shift in genetic medicine. While first-generation gene editors like CRISPR-Cas9 function as "molecular scissors," they are an inherently disruptive tool. By creating double-strand DNA breaks, they introduce significant risks, including unpredictable insertions, deletions, and large genomic rearrangements. Beam represents "Gene Editing 2.0." Its base editing platform is a "molecular pencil," a precision instrument that chemically rewrites a single "letter" of the genetic code without cutting the DNA backbone. This approach offers a potentially superior safety and precision profile, designed to mitigate the core risks that plague its predecessors. This thesis is no longer just theoretical. Beam achieved a landmark milestone with BEAM-302, providing the first-ever clinical proof-of-concept for in vivo base editing in humans. This "one-and-done" therapy for AATD directly corrects the disease-causing PiZ mutation. Early data is exceptionally strong, showing it achieves the dual therapeutic goal: it halts the production of the toxic liver protein (Z-AAT) while simultaneously restoring the functional, protective lung protein (M-AAT). This establishes a clear best-in-class profile and significantly de-risks the entire liver-directed LNP platform.
